invoice.en: a document listing goods or services provided and the amount owed for them

ex.en: Please send the invoice to our accounting department by Friday.
ex.ko: 금요일까지 청구서를 저희 회계팀으로 보내주세요.

invoice.en: to send someone a bill for goods or services

ex.en: The supplier invoiced the client for the completed work.
ex.ko: 공급업체는 완료된 작업에 대해 고객에게 청구서를 보냈다.

ex.en: An irate customer demanded a refund at the counter.
ex.ko: 몹시 화가 난 고객이 계산대에서 환불을 요구했다.

ex.en: "This is unacceptable!" she said irately.
ex.ko: "이건 용납할 수 없어요!" 그녀가 몹시 화를 내며 말했다.

irony.en: a situation that is strikingly different from, or the opposite of, what would normally be expected

ex.en: It's a bitter irony that the fire station burned down.
ex.ko: 소방서가 불에 탔다는 것은 씁쓸한 아이러니다.

irony.cue: 말한 것과 반대의 뜻을 전달하는 문학 기법
irony.en: a way of using language in which what is said is the opposite of what is meant, often for humorous or critical effect

ex.en: The author uses irony when the character calmly says 'What a wonderful day' during the storm.
ex.ko: 작가는 폭풍우 속에서 등장인물이 태연하게 '정말 멋진 날이야'라고 말할 때 반어법을 사용한다.

isolate.en: to separate someone or something from others

ex.en: The patient was isolated to prevent the infection from spreading.
ex.ko: 감염이 퍼지는 것을 막기 위해 그 환자는 격리되었다.

isolate.en: to obtain a substance in a pure form, separate from everything else

ex.en: Scientists managed to isolate the gene responsible for the disease.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 그 질병을 유발하는 유전자를 분리해내는 데 성공했다.

isolated.en: far away from other places, people, or things

ex.en: They live in an isolated village in the mountains.
ex.ko: 그들은 산속의 외딴 마을에 산다.

isolated.en: feeling alone and without help or support from others

ex.en: She felt increasingly isolated after moving to a new city.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 도시로 이사한 후 점점 더 고립감을 느꼈다.

isolated.en: happening only once and not connected to other similar events

ex.en: The company insists it was an isolated incident, not a pattern.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 이것이 반복되는 패턴이 아니라 단발적인 사건이라고 주장한다.

itinerary.en: a planned route or schedule for a journey

ex.en: Our itinerary includes three days in Rome and two in Florence.
ex.ko: 우리의 여행 일정에는 로마 3일과 피렌체 2일이 포함되어 있다.

jersey.en: a shirt worn by a member of a sports team, usually showing the team's colors and design

ex.en: He proudly wore his team's jersey to the match.
ex.ko: 그는 자랑스럽게 팀 저지를 입고 경기에 갔다.

jersey.en: a soft, stretchy knitted fabric used for clothing

ex.en: The dress is made of a lightweight jersey fabric.
ex.ko: 그 드레스는 가벼운 저지 원단으로 만들어졌다.

jurisdiction.en: the official power to make legal decisions and judgments in a particular area

ex.en: This case falls outside the court's jurisdiction.
ex.ko: 이 사건은 그 법원의 관할권 밖이다.

justly.en: in a fair or deserved way

ex.en: He was justly praised for his courage.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 용기에 대해 정당하게 칭찬받았다.

juvenile.en: a young person who is not yet an adult, especially in a legal context

ex.en: The juvenile court handles cases involving offenders under eighteen.
ex.ko: 소년 법원은 18세 미만 범죄자와 관련된 사건을 다룬다.

juvenile.en: behaving in a silly, childish way that is not appropriate for an adult

ex.en: His juvenile jokes annoyed his colleagues.
ex.ko: 그의 유치한 농담은 동료들을 짜증나게 했다.

knowledgeable.cue: 특정 분야에 대해 아는 것이 많은
knowledgeable.en: having a lot of knowledge or information about a particular subject

ex.en: Our tour guide was extremely knowledgeable about local history.
ex.ko: 우리 여행 가이드는 지역 역사에 대해 매우 박식했다.

ex.en: The team delivered a lackluster performance in the final match.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 결승전에서 활기 없는 경기를 펼쳤다.

ex.en: Her hair looked dry and lackluster after the long flight.
ex.ko: 긴 비행 후 그녀의 머리카락은 건조하고 윤기가 없어 보였다.

lapse.en: a brief failure to remember, pay attention, or behave correctly

ex.en: In a moment of lapse, he forgot his own phone number.
ex.ko: 순간적인 착오로 그는 자신의 전화번호를 잊어버렸다.

lapse.en: (of a contract, license, etc.) to stop being valid because the allowed time has ended

ex.en: Her membership will lapse if she doesn't renew it this month.
ex.ko: 이번 달에 갱신하지 않으면 그녀의 회원 자격은 소멸될 것이다.

lapse.en: a temporary decline from a good standard of behavior or judgment

ex.en: The scandal was seen as a lapse in judgment, not a pattern.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 반복되는 패턴이 아니라 일시적인 판단 착오로 여겨졌다.

latent.en: existing but not yet developed or visible; hidden

ex.en: The therapy helped uncover his latent talent for painting.
ex.ko: 그 치료는 그의 잠재된 그림 재능을 발견하는 데 도움이 되었다.

lavatory.en: a room containing a toilet, especially in a public building or on an airplane

ex.en: Passengers were asked to remain seated while the lavatory was being cleaned.
ex.ko: 화장실을 청소하는 동안 승객들은 자리에 앉아 있으라는 요청을 받았다.

legacy.en: money or property that you receive from someone after they die

ex.en: She used her grandmother's legacy to start a small business.
ex.ko: 그녀는 할머니의 유산으로 작은 사업을 시작했다.

legacy.en: something that remains or is handed down from the past, such as an achievement or reputation

ex.en: The president's legacy includes major reforms in education.
ex.ko: 그 대통령의 유산에는 교육 분야의 주요 개혁이 포함된다.

ex.en: The hot weather made everyone feel lethargic.
ex.ko: 더운 날씨 때문에 모두가 무기력함을 느꼈다.

ex.en: He moved lethargically around the office all afternoon.
ex.ko: 그는 오후 내내 사무실에서 무기력하게 움직였다.

lethargy.en: a lack of energy or enthusiasm

ex.en: A feeling of lethargy overcame her after the long flight.
ex.ko: 긴 비행 후 그녀는 무기력함에 사로잡혔다.

liaison.en: communication and cooperation between different groups or organizations

ex.en: She works in liaison between the marketing and sales teams.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마케팅팀과 영업팀 사이의 연락 업무를 맡고 있다.

liaison.en: a person who acts as a link to help different groups work together

ex.en: He was appointed as the liaison between the police and the local community.
ex.ko: 그는 경찰과 지역사회 간의 연락 담당자로 임명되었다.

likelihood.en: the chance that something will happen; probability

ex.en: There is a strong likelihood that the meeting will be postponed.
ex.ko: 회의가 연기될 가능성이 크다.

ex.en: The desert stretched out with seemingly limitless sand dunes.
ex.ko: 사막은 끝없이 펼쳐진 모래언덕으로 이어졌다.

linear.en: arranged in or extending along a straight line; involving a straightforward sequence

ex.en: The story follows a linear timeline from childhood to old age.
ex.ko: 그 이야기는 어린 시절부터 노년까지 일직선적인 시간 순서를 따른다.

linear.en: (in mathematics) able to be represented by a straight line on a graph; involving quantities that change at a constant rate

ex.en: There is a linear relationship between hours studied and test scores.
ex.ko: 공부한 시간과 시험 점수 사이에는 선형 관계가 있다.

linearly.en: in a straight line or at a constant rate

ex.en: The values increase linearly as the temperature rises.
ex.ko: 온도가 오르면 값은 선형적으로 증가한다.

loom.en: to appear as a large, unclear, and often threatening shape

ex.en: A dark shadow loomed over the valley as the storm approached.
ex.ko: 폭풍이 다가오면서 어두운 그림자가 계곡 위로 어렴풋이 나타났다.

loom.en: (of something worrying) to seem likely to happen soon

ex.en: The threat of layoffs loomed over the entire company.
ex.ko: 해고의 위협이 회사 전체에 드리워져 있었다.

loom.en: a machine used for weaving thread into cloth

ex.en: The weaver worked at her loom late into the night.
ex.ko: 그 직조공은 밤늦게까지 베틀에서 일했다.

ludicrous.en: so unreasonable, foolish, or out of place as to be amusing

ex.en: It's ludicrous to expect us to finish this project in one day.
ex.ko: 이 프로젝트를 하루 만에 끝내라는 건 터무니없다.

ludicrously.en: in a way that is absurdly unreasonable

ex.en: The price of the ticket was ludicrously high.
ex.ko: 그 티켓 가격은 터무니없이 비쌌다.

lust.en: a very strong desire for something

ex.en: His lust for power drove him to betray his closest friends.
ex.ko: 권력에 대한 그의 강한 욕망은 그가 가장 가까운 친구들을 배신하게 만들었다.

ex.en: The novel explores themes of lust and jealousy.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 정욕과 질투라는 주제를 탐구한다.

macabre.en: disturbing and horrifying because concerned with death or violence

ex.en: The museum's macabre exhibit featured medieval torture devices.
ex.ko: 그 박물관의 섬뜩한 전시는 중세 고문 도구들을 다루었다.

magnetic.en: having the power to attract objects made of iron or steel

ex.en: The refrigerator door has a magnetic strip that keeps it closed.
ex.ko: 냉장고 문에는 문을 닫힌 상태로 유지해주는 자석 띠가 있다.

magnetic.en: having a powerful and attractive personal quality that draws people in

ex.en: The speaker had a magnetic personality that captivated the audience.
ex.ko: 그 연사는 청중을 사로잡는 매력적인 개성을 지니고 있었다.

ex.en: The data is stored magnetically on the hard drive.
ex.ko: 데이터는 하드 드라이브에 자기적으로 저장된다.

magnetically.en: in a way that strongly attracts, as if by magnetism

ex.en: She was magnetically drawn to the mysterious stranger.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 신비로운 낯선 사람에게 강하게 끌렸다.

magnetism.en: the physical force that attracts iron and other metals

ex.en: The needle points north due to the Earth's magnetism.
ex.ko: 지구의 자성 때문에 바늘은 북쪽을 가리킨다.

magnetism.en: a powerful personal charm that attracts people

ex.en: The actor's magnetism made every scene memorable.
ex.ko: 그 배우의 매력은 모든 장면을 인상적으로 만들었다.

ex.en: The elevator malfunctioned and got stuck between floors.
ex.ko: 엘리베이터가 오작동해서 층 사이에 멈춰버렸다.

ex.en: The accident was caused by a mechanical malfunction.
ex.ko: 그 사고는 기계 오작동으로 인해 발생했다.

maneuver.en: to move something skillfully or carefully into a particular position

ex.en: She maneuvered the car into a tight parking space.
ex.ko: 그녀는 좁은 주차 공간으로 차를 능숙하게 몰았다.

maneuver.en: a clever plan or action used to gain an advantage

ex.en: His resignation was seen as a political maneuver.
ex.ko: 그의 사임은 정치적 술책으로 여겨졌다.

maneuver.en: a planned movement of military forces, especially as training

ex.en: The troops took part in a large-scale military maneuver.
ex.ko: 그 부대는 대규모 군사 기동훈련에 참가했다.

manicure.en: a cosmetic treatment for the hands and fingernails

ex.en: She treated herself to a manicure before the wedding.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결혼식 전에 손톱 관리를 받았다.

manicure.en: to trim and shape someone's fingernails

ex.en: The salon manicures nails for twenty dollars.
ex.ko: 그 미용실은 20달러에 손톱을 손질해준다.

manipulation.en: the act of controlling or influencing someone or something in a clever or unfair way

ex.en: The scandal revealed years of media manipulation.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 수년간의 언론 조작을 드러냈다.

manipulation.en: the skillful use of the hands to handle or operate something

ex.en: The surgery requires precise manipulation of tiny instruments.
ex.ko: 그 수술은 작은 기구들을 정밀하게 다루는 기술을 필요로 한다.

marginal.en: small in size, degree, or importance; barely significant

ex.en: There was only a marginal improvement in sales this quarter.
ex.ko: 이번 분기에는 매출이 미미하게 개선되었을 뿐이다.

marginal.en: relating to or situated at the edge or a border; not central

ex.en: Farmers struggled to grow crops on marginal land.
ex.ko: 농부들은 척박한 주변 땅에서 작물을 재배하는 데 어려움을 겪었다.

marginally.en: to a very small degree; only slightly

ex.en: Prices rose only marginally last month.
ex.ko: 지난달 물가는 아주 미미하게만 상승했다.

mark-up.en: the amount added to the cost of a product to determine its selling price

ex.en: The store applies a 50% mark-up on imported goods.
ex.ko: 그 매장은 수입품에 50%의 이윤을 붙인다.

marvel.en: something that causes feelings of wonder and admiration

ex.en: The ancient pyramids are considered a marvel of engineering.
ex.ko: 고대 피라미드는 공학의 경이로 여겨진다.

marvel.en: to feel great surprise or admiration

ex.en: Visitors marveled at the view from the mountaintop.
ex.ko: 방문객들은 산꼭대기에서의 전망에 경탄했다.

maternal.en: relating to or typical of a mother, especially in showing love and care

ex.en: She felt a strong maternal instinct to protect the child.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 아이를 보호하려는 강한 모성 본능을 느꼈다.

maternal.en: related through one's mother's side of the family

ex.en: His maternal grandfather was a well-known painter.
ex.ko: 그의 외할아버지는 유명한 화가였다.

maternalistic.en: showing an excessive or controlling desire to care for and protect others, like a mother

ex.en: Her maternalistic attitude toward her employees sometimes felt overbearing.
ex.ko: 직원들에 대한 그녀의 모성적인 태도는 때때로 지나치게 느껴졌다.

meaningless.en: having no meaning, purpose, or value

ex.en: Without her, the celebration felt meaningless.
ex.ko: 그녀 없이는 그 축하 행사가 무의미하게 느껴졌다.

meaningless.en: producing no useful result or effect

ex.en: His apology was meaningless without any real change in behavior.
ex.ko: 행동의 실질적인 변화 없이 그의 사과는 헛된 것이었다.

meaninglessly.en: in a way that has no meaning or purpose

ex.en: The hours passed meaninglessly as he stared at the wall.
ex.ko: 그가 벽만 바라보는 동안 시간은 무의미하게 흘러갔다.

mechanism.en: a part of a machine that does a particular job, or the way a machine works

ex.en: The clock's mechanism was broken and needed repair.
ex.ko: 그 시계의 장치가 고장 나서 수리가 필요했다.

mechanism.en: a system or process by which something works or is achieved

ex.en: The brain has a natural mechanism for coping with stress.
ex.ko: 뇌에는 스트레스에 대처하는 자연적인 기제가 있다.

methodical.en: done in a careful, orderly, and systematic way

ex.en: The detective took a methodical approach to solving the case.
ex.ko: 그 형사는 사건을 해결하는 데 체계적인 접근법을 취했다.

midwife.en: a person trained to help women give birth

ex.en: The midwife stayed with her throughout the entire delivery.
ex.ko: 그 조산사는 출산 내내 그녀 곁을 지켰다.

ex.en: The smell of coffee mingled with the scent of fresh bread.
ex.ko: 커피 향이 갓 구운 빵 냄새와 뒤섞였다.

mingle.en: to move around and talk to different people at a social event

ex.en: Guests mingled in the garden before dinner.
ex.ko: 손님들은 저녁 식사 전에 정원에서 서로 어울렸다.

minutiae.en: small, precise details that are often unimportant

ex.en: He was known for obsessing over the minutiae of every contract.
ex.ko: 그는 모든 계약서의 사소한 세부사항에 집착하는 것으로 유명했다.

misplace.en: to put something somewhere and then forget where it is

ex.en: I think I misplaced my keys somewhere in the office.
ex.ko: 사무실 어딘가에 열쇠를 잘못 둔 것 같다.

misplace.en: to give trust, love, or confidence to someone or something that does not deserve it

ex.en: Her trust in him turned out to be misplaced.
ex.ko: 그에 대한 그녀의 신뢰는 잘못된 것으로 드러났다.

monotonous.en: boring because it stays the same and does not change

ex.en: He grew tired of his monotonous daily routine.
ex.ko: 그는 단조로운 일상에 지쳐갔다.

monotonous.en: (of a sound or voice) staying at the same pitch without variation

ex.en: The lecturer spoke in a monotonous voice that put students to sleep.
ex.ko: 그 강사는 단조로운 목소리로 말해서 학생들을 졸게 만들었다.

monotonously.en: in a way that does not change and is boring

ex.en: The machine hummed monotonously in the background.
ex.ko: 그 기계는 배경에서 단조롭게 웅웅거렸다.

monotony.en: the quality of being boring because it never changes

ex.en: She took up painting to break the monotony of retirement.
ex.ko: 그녀는 은퇴 생활의 단조로움을 깨려고 그림을 시작했다.

motley.en: consisting of a varied, often oddly mixed group of different kinds

ex.en: A motley crew of volunteers showed up to help with the cleanup.
ex.ko: 각양각색의 자원봉사자 무리가 청소를 도우러 나타났다.

mountaineer.en: a person who climbs mountains, especially as a sport

ex.en: The experienced mountaineer reached the summit despite the storm.
ex.ko: 그 노련한 등산가는 폭풍우 속에서도 정상에 도달했다.

munch.en: to eat something steadily, making a chewing noise

ex.en: The kids sat munching on popcorn during the movie.
ex.ko: 아이들은 영화를 보는 동안 팝콘을 우적우적 먹었다.

ex.en: The athlete had a muscular build from years of training.
ex.ko: 그 운동선수는 수년간의 훈련으로 근육질의 몸을 갖게 되었다.

muscular.en: relating to or affecting the muscles

ex.en: She suffered a muscular injury during the marathon.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마라톤 도중 근육 부상을 입었다.

mutually.en: in a way that is shared or felt equally by two or more people

ex.en: The two companies reached a mutually beneficial agreement.
ex.ko: 그 두 회사는 상호 이익이 되는 합의에 도달했다.

mythological.en: relating to or appearing in traditional myths

ex.en: The statue depicts a mythological creature, half-man and half-horse.
ex.ko: 그 조각상은 반은 사람, 반은 말인 신화 속 생물을 묘사한다.

mythology.en: a collection of traditional stories, especially about gods and heroes

ex.en: Greek mythology has inspired countless works of art and literature.
ex.ko: 그리스 신화는 수많은 미술과 문학 작품에 영감을 주었다.

ex.en: She is writing her thesis on comparative mythology.
ex.ko: 그녀는 비교 신화학에 관한 논문을 쓰고 있다.

naive.en: lacking experience or judgment, and too willing to believe things

ex.en: It was naive of him to trust a stranger with his money.
ex.ko: 낯선 사람에게 돈을 맡긴 것은 그가 순진했던 것이다.

naively.en: in a naive or overly trusting way

ex.en: She naively believed his every promise.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 모든 약속을 순진하게 믿었다.

naivety.en: a lack of experience or wisdom, leading someone to trust too easily

ex.en: His naivety made him an easy target for scammers.
ex.ko: 그의 순진함 때문에 그는 사기꾼들의 쉬운 표적이 되었다.

naturalness.en: the quality of being natural, relaxed, and not forced or artificial

ex.en: The actress's naturalness on screen made her instantly likable.
ex.ko: 그 여배우의 화면 속 자연스러움이 그녀를 단번에 호감형으로 만들었다.

neglectful.en: not giving enough care or attention to someone or something

ex.en: The parents were accused of being neglectful toward their children.
ex.ko: 그 부모는 자녀를 소홀히 대했다는 비난을 받았다.

negligent.en: failing to give proper care or attention, especially in a way that causes harm

ex.en: The hospital was found negligent in the patient's treatment.
ex.ko: 그 병원은 환자 치료에 있어 태만한 것으로 밝혀졌다.

negligently.en: in a way that shows a lack of proper care or attention

ex.en: The equipment was negligently left unattended near the pool.
ex.ko: 그 장비는 수영장 근처에 부주의하게 방치되어 있었다.

negligible.en: so small or unimportant that it is not worth considering

ex.en: The difference in price was negligible, so we picked the closer store.
ex.ko: 가격 차이가 미미해서 우리는 더 가까운 가게를 골랐다.

neutralization.en: the act of making something harmless or ineffective

ex.en: The vaccine works through the neutralization of the virus.
ex.ko: 그 백신은 바이러스의 무력화를 통해 작용한다.

neutralization.en: a chemical reaction in which an acid and a base react to form a salt

ex.en: The neutralization of the acid produced a harmless salt solution.
ex.ko: 그 산의 중화는 무해한 염 용액을 만들어냈다.

neutralize.en: to stop something from having any effect or power

ex.en: Baking soda can neutralize strong odors in the fridge.
ex.ko: 베이킹소다는 냉장고 안의 강한 냄새를 없앨 수 있다.

neutralize.en: to make a substance chemically neutral, neither acidic nor basic

ex.en: Lime is often used to neutralize acidic soil.
ex.ko: 석회는 산성 토양을 중화하는 데 자주 사용된다.

niche.en: a position or activity that particularly suits a person's abilities or character

ex.en: After years of trying different jobs, she finally found her niche as a designer.
ex.ko: 여러 직업을 시도한 끝에 그녀는 마침내 디자이너로서 자신의 적합한 자리를 찾았다.

niche.en: a specialized market for a particular kind of product or service

ex.en: The company built a successful business by targeting a niche market.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 틈새시장을 공략해 성공적인 사업을 일구었다.

nocturnal.en: active at night rather than during the day

ex.en: Owls are nocturnal birds that hunt in the dark.
ex.ko: 부엉이는 어둠 속에서 사냥하는 야행성 새다.

ex.en: The suspect was described as a nondescript man in a gray coat.
ex.ko: 그 용의자는 회색 코트를 입은 특징 없는 남자로 묘사되었다.

norms.en: standards of behavior that are considered normal or acceptable in a society

ex.en: The new generation is challenging traditional social norms.
ex.ko: 새로운 세대는 전통적인 사회 규범에 도전하고 있다.

notable.en: important or interesting enough to deserve attention

ex.en: There has been a notable increase in remote work since the pandemic.
ex.ko: 팬데믹 이후 재택근무가 눈에 띄게 증가했다.

ex.en: Several notable scientists attended the conference.
ex.ko: 여러 저명한 과학자들이 그 학회에 참석했다.

notwithstanding.en: in spite of; without being affected by

ex.en: Notwithstanding the heavy rain, the outdoor concert went ahead as planned.
ex.ko: 폭우에도 불구하고 야외 콘서트는 예정대로 진행되었다.

numb.en: unable to feel anything in a part of your body, often because of cold or an injury

ex.en: My fingers went numb from the cold.
ex.ko: 손가락이 추위로 감각이 없어졌다.

numb.cue: 충격을 받아 아무 감정도 못 느끼는
numb.en: unable to feel or show emotion, especially because of shock

ex.en: She felt numb with grief after hearing the news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 슬픔에 멍해졌다.

numb.en: to make a part of the body, or a person's feelings, unable to sense anything

ex.en: The dentist numbed my gum before the procedure.
ex.ko: 치과의사는 시술 전에 내 잇몸을 마비시켰다.

oblige.en: to force someone to do something because of a rule, law, or need

ex.en: The contract obliges both parties to give 30 days' notice.
ex.ko: 그 계약은 양측 모두에게 30일 전 통지 의무를 지운다.

oblige.en: to do something to help someone or to please them

ex.en: Happy to oblige — I'll send the documents right away.
ex.ko: 기꺼이 도와드리죠 — 서류를 바로 보내드릴게요.

ex.en: The film was based on an obscure novel from the 1930s.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 1930년대의 잘 알려지지 않은 소설을 바탕으로 했다.

obscure.en: not clearly expressed or easy to understand

ex.en: His explanation was so obscure that nobody understood it.
ex.ko: 그의 설명은 너무 모호해서 아무도 이해하지 못했다.

obscure.en: to make something difficult to see or understand

ex.en: Thick clouds obscured the view of the mountains.
ex.ko: 짙은 구름이 산의 경치를 가렸다.

obscurely.en: in a way that is unclear or hard to understand

ex.en: He answered the question obscurely, avoiding a direct response.
ex.ko: 그는 직접적인 답을 피하며 모호하게 질문에 답했다.

occupant.en: a person who lives in or uses a particular place

ex.en: The current occupant of the apartment has lived there for ten years.
ex.ko: 그 아파트의 현재 거주자는 그곳에서 10년째 살고 있다.

occupant.en: a person who is inside a vehicle at a particular time

ex.en: All occupants of the car were wearing seatbelts.
ex.ko: 차량의 모든 탑승자는 안전벨트를 매고 있었다.

occupational.en: relating to a person's job or profession

ex.en: Nurses face a higher risk of occupational injuries.
ex.ko: 간호사들은 직업적 부상의 위험이 더 높다.

oddity.en: the quality of being strange or unusual

ex.en: The oddity of his behavior made his coworkers uneasy.
ex.ko: 그의 행동이 보이는 기이함이 동료들을 불편하게 만들었다.

oddity.en: a person or thing that is strange or unusual

ex.en: The two-headed calf was displayed as a rare oddity.
ex.ko: 그 머리 둘 달린 송아지는 희귀한 진기품으로 전시되었다.

offspring.en: a person's child, or an animal's baby

ex.en: The couple raised their offspring in a small farmhouse.
ex.ko: 그 부부는 자녀를 작은 농가에서 길렀다.

orchard.en: a piece of land where fruit trees are grown

ex.en: We picked apples at a nearby orchard last weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 지난 주말 근처 과수원에서 사과를 땄다.

ex.en: Ordinarily, I would walk to work, but today it was raining.
ex.ko: 평소에는 걸어서 출근하지만 오늘은 비가 왔다.

organizational.en: relating to the way an organization is planned or structured

ex.en: The company went through major organizational changes last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 큰 조직 개편을 겪었다.

ornamental.en: made or grown to be decorative rather than useful

ex.en: They planted ornamental flowers along the garden path.
ex.ko: 그들은 정원 오솔길을 따라 장식용 꽃을 심었다.

ornamental.en: a plant grown for its beauty rather than for food or other use

ex.en: The nursery sells a wide variety of ornamentals.
ex.ko: 그 묘목장은 다양한 관상용 식물을 판매한다.

otter.en: a small furry animal with webbed feet that lives near water and eats fish

ex.en: A playful otter slid down the muddy riverbank.
ex.ko: 장난기 많은 수달이 진흙투성이 강둑을 미끄러져 내려갔다.

outset.en: the beginning or start of something

ex.en: From the outset, the project faced serious funding problems.
ex.ko: 처음부터 그 프로젝트는 심각한 자금 문제에 직면했다.

overalls.en: a loose garment with a bib and straps, worn over other clothes for work or as casual wear

ex.en: The mechanic wore grease-stained overalls.
ex.ko: 그 정비공은 기름때가 묻은 작업복을 입고 있었다.

overdraft.en: an amount of money that you owe the bank because you have taken out more than you had in your account

ex.en: He had to pay a fee for going into overdraft.
ex.ko: 그는 당좌 대월에 대한 수수료를 내야 했다.

oversee.en: to watch and direct a project, activity, or group of people

ex.en: She was hired to oversee the construction of the new building.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 건물의 건설을 감독하기 위해 고용되었다.

overseer.en: a person whose job is to supervise workers or a project

ex.en: The overseer made sure every task was completed on schedule.
ex.ko: 그 감독관은 모든 작업이 일정대로 완료되도록 확인했다.

panorama.en: a wide view of an area of land

ex.en: From the tower, you can see a stunning panorama of the city.
ex.ko: 그 탑에서는 도시의 멋진 전경을 볼 수 있다.

panoramic.en: giving a wide view of a large area

ex.en: The hotel room offers a panoramic view of the ocean.
ex.ko: 그 호텔 객실은 바다의 파노라마 전망을 제공한다.

parasite.en: an animal or plant that lives on or in another living thing and gets food from it

ex.en: The parasite lives inside the host's intestines.
ex.ko: 그 기생충은 숙주의 장 안에 산다.

parasite.en: a person who takes advantage of others without giving anything back

ex.en: He was criticized as a parasite living off his family's wealth.
ex.ko: 그는 가족의 재산에 빌붙어 사는 기생충 같은 존재라고 비난받았다.

paternal.en: relating to or typical of a father, especially in showing care

ex.en: He gave the young intern some paternal advice.
ex.ko: 그는 젊은 인턴에게 아버지 같은 조언을 해주었다.

paternal.en: related through one's father's side of the family

ex.en: Her paternal grandmother taught her how to bake bread.
ex.ko: 그녀의 친할머니는 그녀에게 빵 굽는 법을 가르쳐주었다.

paternalistic.en: controlling people's lives in a way that limits their freedom, while claiming to act in their best interest

ex.en: Critics called the policy paternalistic, saying it treated citizens like children.
ex.ko: 비평가들은 그 정책이 시민들을 아이처럼 대한다며 가부장적이라고 비판했다.

pathos.en: a quality in something that makes people feel sadness or sympathy

ex.en: The film's ending was full of pathos, leaving the audience in tears.
ex.ko: 그 영화의 결말은 비애감으로 가득 차 관객들을 눈물짓게 했다.

paywall.cue: 돈을 내야 볼 수 있는 온라인 콘텐츠
paywall.en: a system that prevents internet users from accessing certain online content unless they pay

ex.en: The article is behind a paywall, so you need a subscription to read it.
ex.ko: 그 기사는 유료 장벽 뒤에 있어서 읽으려면 구독이 필요하다.

peacock.en: a large bird with a long, colorful tail that it can spread out like a fan

ex.en: The peacock spread its feathers to attract a mate.
ex.ko: 그 공작새는 짝을 유혹하기 위해 깃털을 펼쳤다.

peasant.cue: 옛날 작은 땅을 일구던 가난한 농부
peasant.en: a poor farmer who owns or rents a small amount of land, especially in the past

ex.en: In medieval times, most peasants worked the land for wealthy landowners.
ex.ko: 중세시대에 대부분의 소작농들은 부유한 지주를 위해 땅을 경작했다.

pedicure.en: a cosmetic treatment for the feet and toenails

ex.en: She got a pedicure before going on vacation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 휴가를 가기 전에 발 관리를 받았다.

pedicure.en: to give someone a cosmetic treatment for their feet and toenails

ex.en: The nail salon pedicures feet as well as manicures hands.
ex.ko: 그 네일샵은 손 관리뿐 아니라 발 관리도 해준다.

ex.en: The willow tree's pendulous branches swayed in the breeze.
ex.ko: 그 버드나무의 축 늘어진 가지가 산들바람에 흔들렸다.

pendulum.en: a weight hung from a fixed point that swings freely back and forth

ex.en: The pendulum of the old clock swung steadily back and forth.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 시계의 추가 꾸준히 좌우로 흔들렸다.

peninsula.en: a piece of land that is almost surrounded by water but is connected to a larger landmass

ex.en: Korea is located on a peninsula in East Asia.
ex.ko: 한국은 동아시아의 한 반도에 위치해 있다.

perceptive.en: quick to notice or understand things that are not obvious

ex.en: Her perceptive comments helped the team see the problem differently.
ex.ko: 그녀의 통찰력 있는 의견은 팀이 문제를 다르게 보도록 도와주었다.

perceptively.en: in a way that shows a quick or clear understanding of things

ex.en: He perceptively pointed out the flaw in the argument.
ex.ko: 그는 그 주장의 결함을 통찰력 있게 지적했다.

perpetuate.en: to cause something, especially something bad, to continue for a long time

ex.en: The policy only perpetuates the cycle of poverty.
ex.ko: 그 정책은 빈곤의 악순환을 지속시킬 뿐이다.

persecute.en: to treat someone cruelly and unfairly over a long period of time, especially because of their race, religion, or beliefs

ex.en: The minority group was persecuted for its religious beliefs.
ex.ko: 그 소수 집단은 종교적 신념 때문에 박해받았다.

persecution.cue: 인종·종교 등을 이유로 한 잔인한 처사
persecution.en: cruel and unfair treatment of a person or group over a long period of time, especially because of their race, religion, or beliefs

ex.en: Many refugees fled their country to escape political persecution.
ex.ko: 많은 난민들이 정치적 박해를 피해 조국을 떠났다.

personalization.en: the process of making something suited to the needs or preferences of a particular person

ex.en: The app allows a high level of personalization for each user.
ex.ko: 그 앱은 각 사용자에게 높은 수준의 개인화를 제공한다.

personalize.en: to design or change something to suit the needs or tastes of a particular person

ex.en: You can personalize your phone case with your own photos.
ex.ko: 자신의 사진으로 휴대폰 케이스를 맞춤화할 수 있다.

persuasively.en: in a way that makes someone likely to believe or do something

ex.en: She argued persuasively for more funding in education.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교육에 더 많은 예산을 배정해야 한다고 설득력 있게 주장했다.

pertain to.en: to be relevant or related to something

ex.en: This rule pertains to all employees, regardless of position.
ex.ko: 이 규칙은 직급에 관계없이 모든 직원에게 적용된다.

pertinent.en: directly relevant to the matter being discussed

ex.en: Please provide any pertinent information about your medical history.
ex.ko: 귀하의 병력에 관한 관련 정보를 제공해 주세요.

ex.en: The young pianist gave a phenomenal performance at the concert.
ex.ko: 그 젊은 피아니스트는 연주회에서 경이로운 연주를 선보였다.

phenomenally.en: to a remarkably or extremely great degree

ex.en: The new product has been phenomenally successful.
ex.ko: 그 신제품은 놀랍도록 성공적이었다.

physicality.en: the quality of being physically strong, active, or noticeably present in the body

ex.en: The dancer's physicality brought raw energy to every performance.
ex.ko: 그 무용수의 신체성은 모든 공연에 원초적인 에너지를 불어넣었다.

physique.en: the shape and size of a person's body

ex.en: Years of training gave him an athletic physique.
ex.ko: 수년간의 훈련은 그에게 탄탄한 체격을 만들어주었다.

pigeon.en: a common gray bird often seen in cities

ex.en: Pigeons gathered in the square looking for crumbs.
ex.ko: 비둘기들이 부스러기를 찾아 광장에 모여들었다.

pinnacle.en: the highest point of achievement or success

ex.en: Winning the championship was the pinnacle of her career.
ex.ko: 챔피언십에서 우승한 것은 그녀 경력의 정점이었다.

pinnacle.en: a tall, pointed piece of rock, such as a mountain peak

ex.en: Climbers finally reached the icy pinnacle after days of effort.
ex.ko: 등반가들은 며칠간의 노력 끝에 마침내 얼음 덮인 봉우리에 도달했다.

pioneer.en: a person who is among the first to explore or develop a new area, method, or idea

ex.en: She was a pioneer in the field of computer science.
ex.ko: 그녀는 컴퓨터 과학 분야의 선구자였다.

pioneer.en: to be the first to develop or use a new method, area, or idea

ex.en: The company pioneered a new technique for recycling plastic.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 플라스틱 재활용을 위한 새로운 기술을 개척했다.

placid.en: (of water) calm, with no big waves or movement

ex.en: The lake was placid in the early morning light.
ex.ko: 이른 아침 햇살 속에서 그 호수는 잔잔했다.

placid.en: (of a person) calm and not easily upset or angered

ex.en: Despite the chaos around her, she remained placid.
ex.ko: 주변이 혼란스러웠지만 그녀는 평온함을 유지했다.

placidly.en: in a calm and untroubled manner

ex.en: The cat slept placidly in the sun all afternoon.
ex.ko: 그 고양이는 오후 내내 햇볕 속에서 평온하게 잤다.

plaque.en: a flat piece of metal or stone fixed to a wall as a memorial or to mark something

ex.en: A plaque on the wall commemorates the building's founder.
ex.ko: 벽에 걸린 명판은 그 건물의 설립자를 기린다.

plaque.en: a thin, harmful film that forms on teeth

ex.en: Brushing regularly helps remove plaque from your teeth.
ex.ko: 규칙적인 양치질은 치아의 치태를 제거하는 데 도움이 된다.

plaster.en: a soft mixture that becomes hard when it dries, used to cover walls and ceilings

ex.en: Cracks appeared in the plaster after the earthquake.
ex.ko: 지진 후 회반죽에 균열이 생겼다.

plaster.en: to cover a wall or surface thickly with something

ex.en: Posters were plastered all over the wall.
ex.ko: 포스터들이 벽 전체에 덕지덕지 붙어 있었다.

plausibility.en: the quality of seeming likely to be true or reasonable

ex.en: Investigators questioned the plausibility of his alibi.
ex.ko: 수사관들은 그의 알리바이의 타당성에 의문을 제기했다.

plausible.en: seeming likely to be true or reasonable

ex.en: She gave a plausible explanation for why she was late.
ex.ko: 그녀는 왜 늦었는지 그럴듯한 설명을 했다.

plausibly.en: in a way that seems likely to be true or reasonable

ex.en: He plausibly argued that the delay was unavoidable.
ex.ko: 그는 그 지연이 불가피했다고 그럴듯하게 주장했다.

pleasurable.en: giving a feeling of pleasure or enjoyment

ex.en: Reading before bed is a pleasurable way to relax.
ex.ko: 자기 전에 책을 읽는 것은 즐거운 휴식 방법이다.

ploy.en: an action or plan intended to gain an advantage, often in a deceptive way

ex.en: Critics called the announcement a publicity ploy.
ex.ko: 비평가들은 그 발표를 홍보용 술책이라고 불렀다.

plumbing.cue: 건물 안 물이 흐르는 관 설비
plumbing.en: the system of pipes that carries water and gas through a building

ex.en: The old house needed all-new plumbing.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 집은 배관을 전부 새로 해야 했다.

plumbing.en: the work of installing and repairing pipes and fixtures

ex.en: He learned plumbing from his father.
ex.ko: 그는 아버지에게서 배관 일을 배웠다.

plummet.en: to fall or drop suddenly and steeply

ex.en: Stock prices plummeted after the announcement.
ex.ko: 그 발표 이후 주가가 급락했다.

plunge.en: to jump or fall suddenly into water or a deep space

ex.en: He plunged into the cold lake without hesitation.
ex.ko: 그는 망설임 없이 차가운 호수로 뛰어들었다.

plunge.en: (of a value or amount) to decrease suddenly and sharply

ex.en: Temperatures plunged below freezing overnight.
ex.ko: 밤사이 기온이 영하로 급락했다.

plunge.en: a sudden and steep fall or decrease

ex.en: The company suffered a sharp plunge in profits.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 이익이 급격히 급락하는 것을 겪었다.

poetess.en: a female poet (an older term, now often replaced by 'poet')

ex.en: The 19th-century poetess wrote under a male pen name.
ex.ko: 그 19세기 여류 시인은 남성 필명으로 글을 썼다.

poise.en: a calm and confident manner, especially under pressure

ex.en: She answered the difficult question with great poise.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 어려운 질문에 대단한 침착함으로 답했다.

poise.en: to be balanced or held still, ready to move

ex.en: The diver poised on the edge of the board before jumping.
ex.ko: 그 다이버는 뛰어들기 전 다이빙대 가장자리에서 자세를 잡고 정지해 있었다.

popularization.en: the process of making something understandable or appealing to ordinary people

ex.en: The book contributed to the popularization of science among general readers.
ex.ko: 그 책은 일반 독자들 사이에서 과학의 대중화에 기여했다.

portable.en: designed to be easily carried or moved

ex.en: She bought a portable speaker for the camping trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 캠핑 여행을 위해 휴대용 스피커를 샀다.

portray.en: to describe or show someone or something in a particular way, especially in writing, art, or film

ex.en: The novel portrays life in a small fishing village.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 작은 어촌 마을의 삶을 묘사한다.

portray.en: to play a character in a film or play

ex.en: The actor portrayed a struggling artist in his latest film.
ex.ko: 그 배우는 최신 영화에서 고군분투하는 예술가를 연기했다.

portrayal.en: the way someone or something is described or represented, especially in art or writing

ex.en: Critics praised the film's realistic portrayal of war.
ex.ko: 평론가들은 그 영화의 사실적인 전쟁 묘사를 칭찬했다.

portrayal.en: an actor's performance of a particular character

ex.en: Her portrayal of the queen won several awards.
ex.ko: 그녀의 여왕 연기는 여러 상을 수상했다.

possessor.en: a person who owns or has something

ex.en: As the possessor of the only key, he was responsible for locking up.
ex.ko: 유일한 열쇠의 소유자로서 그는 문단속을 책임졌다.

posture.en: the way a person holds and positions their body

ex.en: Sitting for long hours can lead to poor posture.
ex.ko: 오랜 시간 앉아 있으면 자세가 나빠질 수 있다.

posture.en: to behave in a way that is meant to impress others rather than being sincere

ex.en: Politicians often posture for the cameras during debates.
ex.ko: 정치인들은 토론 중에 카메라 앞에서 종종 허세를 부린다.

pounce.en: to suddenly jump on or attack someone or something

ex.en: The cat pounced on the mouse without warning.
ex.ko: 그 고양이는 예고 없이 쥐에게 달려들었다.

preach.en: to give a religious talk, especially in a church

ex.en: The pastor preached about forgiveness on Sunday.
ex.ko: 그 목사는 일요일에 용서에 대해 설교했다.

preach.en: to try to persuade people to accept your moral or political views, often in an annoying way

ex.en: He's always preaching about the importance of exercise.
ex.ko: 그는 항상 운동의 중요성에 대해 설파한다.

preacher.en: a person, especially a religious leader, who gives sermons

ex.en: The preacher delivered a moving sermon at the funeral.
ex.ko: 그 설교자는 장례식에서 감동적인 설교를 했다.

precision.en: the quality of being exact and accurate

ex.en: The surgeon performed the operation with remarkable precision.
ex.ko: 그 외과의사는 놀라운 정밀함으로 수술을 수행했다.

predecessor.en: a person who held a job or position before the person who has it now

ex.en: The new CEO praised her predecessor's achievements.
ex.ko: 새 CEO는 전임자의 업적을 칭찬했다.

predecessor.en: a thing, such as a product or system, that existed before another and that it has replaced

ex.en: This smartphone model is faster than its predecessor.
ex.ko: 이 스마트폰 모델은 이전 모델보다 더 빠르다.
